And the downstream effect is still being felt. Most hormone reference ranges used in labs today were established from predominantly male study populations. So when a woman's ferritin, testosterone, or thyroid markers are evaluated against those ranges, the "normal" flag may mean nothing about what is actually optimal for her biology. 1993 was a start, but the lab ranges haven't fully caught up.

Two problems with FunctionHealth. Their analysis and customer support is heavily AI based. They also renew your account without telling you the money is coming out. Good luck trying to speak to an actual person.
And why did you exclude progesterone from your annual test? It is only available as an add-on. For menopausal women that is important to check.

**yt women. Black and brown women are still excluded from much of current testing, and there are significant disparities between treatment and health outcomes that need to be addressed. We still have so far to go.

caption:
You can be iron-deficient with perfect iron levels. Standard labs check iron. They skip the number that actually matters.

Ferritin is what tells you how much your body actually has in reserve. And most standard panels don’t test it. 

Low ferritin drives fatigue, hair loss, brain fog, and poor sleep long before your standard bloodwork raises a flag. And by the time iron “looks off”, you’ve often been depleted for months.

We’re excited to share we’ll be heading to Biohackers World LA alongside an incredible lineup of visionary experts and wellness pioneers in the biohacking space.

As an event-exclusive, we’ll be offering on-site blood draws alongside a complimentary Gencove DNA test kit, bringing together biomarker-level insights with low-pass genome-wide screening and polygenic risk scores across 9 health areas.

Your genetics reveal what you’re predisposed to and paired with bloodwork, it can shift what “in range” actually means for you and help guide more precise, personalized decisions.

caption: in your 30s/40s? you need to listen to this…

We’re the first generation to see diseases showing up earlier, and it’s not a coincidence. It’s because of the environment we grew up in.

Colorectal cancer is now rising fastest in adults under 50. But why?

Ultra-processed foods became the norm in the American diet.  Remote work, screens, and hours of sitting replaced our everyday movement. And preventive screening hasn’t kept pace with how fast risk is shifting.

So what can you actually do?
Start by understanding your baseline and get the data to know what’s happening inside your body from your inflammation, gut, heart, lipids, metabolism and more. Once you test your baseline, you’ll be able to make targeted changes in your lifestyle slowly that gradually improve your long-term health over time

caption:
1 in 3 people will develop cancer in their lifetime. But most won’t see it coming until the cancer has already advanced.

Even with normal labs, no symptoms, or even no family history of cancer, it can still hide quietly in your body without you ever realizing it.

We’re proud to announce the launch of the Galleri® Multi-Cancer Early Detection Test that can identify signals of over 50+ cancer types through a simple blood draw, helping you act early before symptoms show. 

This technology detects for DNA fragments circulating in your blood to catch a potential cancer signal long before it can even show up on a standard lab or routine checkup.

With cancer, timing matters. The earlier it’s found, the more choices you have and the more life you get to live. At Mito, our mission has always been to give people a deeper, earlier, and more proactive understanding of their health. Partnering with Galleri alongside our 100+ biomarkers panel brings us one step closer to that vision.

Now available at exclusive pricing with Mito Health.

caption:
If you've been writing off bad sleep as just part of life, this one's worth reading.

Poor sleep raises inflammatory markers like hsCRP and IL-6, and that chronic inflammation is one of the main drivers of brain aging.

The good news? Inflammation is addressable.

Here are 6 habits, built around your biology, that lower it and protect your brain while you sleep.

Swipe to learn, then retest with Superpower to track your progress.
firstComment: Great list! Do you recommend glasses that block blue light if on phone? Or what’s your recommendation overall for this one?

latestComments.text: Great list! Do you recommend glasses that block blue light if on phone? Or what’s your recommendation overall for this one?
These are scammers! Dont waste your time and monies! Expensive. Oh and 14 tubes needed for their labs. Not 1! Deceitful advertising.

The accelerated aging mechanism is fascinating — chronic sleep debt elevates cortisol chronically, and cortisol directly breaks down collagen and elastin, the two proteins responsible for skin firmness and elasticity. On top of that, growth hormone — which is responsible for cellular repair and tissue regeneration — is released almost exclusively during slow-wave deep sleep. So every night of poor sleep is literally a night your body couldn’t repair itself properly. The compounding effect of that over months and years is what shows up on your face and in your biology.
Magnesium timing & dosage makes a significant difference — most people take it at the wrong time, and  wrong dose

All of this makes sense.
But most people don’t struggle with what to do, it’s just doing it consistently.
@puralyte to track your supplements 😍

Most people don't know what "peak performance" feels like when you get good sleep, but once you experience it, you'll never want to jeopardize sleep again. Made me quit alcohol for good with some exceptions on special occasions
Been wanting to know my bio age since forever
So true! The night is our repair window.  When it’s cut short, repair doesn’t finish and aging accelerates . Sleep is foundational.
This is so important! We normalize bad sleep way too easily, but it really does ripple into inflammation and long-term brain health

caption:
As a mom, you’re pulled in every direction, every day. And the last thing on your mind is your own health. What you may not realize, is that this sacrifice makes it almost impossible to show up everyday as your best self.

Motherhood changes your body at every stage. Postpartum depletes your nutrient stores. Your 30s and 40s bring hormonal shifts most checkups never catch. And after 50, your cardiovascular rid and bone loss accelerates.

As our Care Team Lead and a mother of two, Rhonda Collins, FNP-C, curated three precision panels designed for each stage of motherhood to reflect the real changes your body is going through.

This Mother’s Day, give Mom the gift of personal care that helps her take of herself the way she takes care of everyone else.

3.4 years younger. Not by accident, but through a fasting-mimicking diet thoughtfully measured in real time. 

Not through supplements or expensive clinics. But with a personalized protocol built around your biology and baseline.

Anyone can follow a fasting protocol. Very few can tell you how your body is reacting. Which markers actually moved. Whether it’s even working.

Mito helps close that gap with real-time data, personalized protocols, and a targeted measurement layer that’s built around you.

caption:
HbA1c is your 3-month blood sugar average.

Glucose sticks to your red blood cells and stays there. The higher your blood sugar, the more sticks.

What can help lower it? Consider:
 • Eating in an 8-hour window
 • Starting with fiber and protein
 • Taking short walks after meals

Consistency matters. 

Test HbA1c with Function and see patterns, not snapshots.
firstComment:
Living with diabetes for 10 years now and wow does it make a difference in how you eat and what you do afterwards. More fibre and veg with meals, and eat the veg first, then your protein and starch/carbs last to slow absorbing in blood stream and prevent spikes. 
Go for a walk after meals. Even just 15 minutes. I walk my dog and it helps me get gas out and prevents me from getting sleepy after a meal. 
Recently I heard simply flexing your muscles like little calf raises while under your desk can help your body burn sugars. But I love my walks, rain or shine.

caption:
Prostate cancer is the second leading cause of death in men, but when caught early, it’s one of the most treatable.

1 in 8 men will be diagnosed with prostate cancer in their lifetime. But early detection can shift the odds entirely. We’ve seen it firsthand: 60% of men who’ve tested with Mito show early risk indicators that they might have otherwise missed.

Early detection isn’t just about testing.
It’s your second chance at prevention.

Traditional medicine often starts too late, often only once symptoms surface or a diagnosis is already made.

But prevention starts earlier. Getting a full comprehensive workup early gives you a true baseline of your health, so you can see what’s improving or declining over time.

Your baseline is your starting point.

caption:
Everyone’s doing it. Should you?

GLP-1s aren’t niche anymore. Nearly 12% of U.S. adults have tried one. And that number is about to skyrocket.

Why?

GLP-1s used to live in wellness clinics, Reddit threads, and the group chats of celebrities and the elites.

Now they’re spilling into everyday culture, fueled by a shift in how they’re used: microdosing.

Microdosing isn’t about rapid weight loss or extremes.

It’s about smaller, compounding benefits, without the heavy side effects:
• appetite control
• fewer mental negotiations with food
• steadier energy and blood sugar
• less inflammation
• cardiovascular health

This shift makes GLP-1s more sustainable, and far more desirable.

And when demand increases, supply follows.*

In five years, the question won’t be if you use GLP-1s, but how.

They won’t feel fringe. They’ll feel boring and familiar, as common as a probiotic or creatine.

_
* If you’re exploring GLP-1s, sourcing matters. Use only clinician-prescribed products from licensed, FDA-registered pharmacies with verified purity testing.

replies.text: @_lucasacohen it's so insane the connection! It only starts to be a concern if you are having 3+ cups of unfiltered coffee a day, but worth looking into if you've got unexpected high LDL levels.

@_lucasacohen I had no idea either but strange she warns against it since high LDL is not bad per se, it’s only that a high-sugar diet makes it so. The “cholesterol-myth” of LDL being the villain in causing cardiovascular disease has been long debunked. It’s the high amount of added sugar in your diet that’s causing inflammation of the arteries, plaque build-up, inflammatory VLDL particles, etc. So enjoy your coffee ☕️ but skip the sugar

caption: Supplementing without testing? You’re just flushing your money, time, and effort away. Testing shows you what your body actually needs so you can supplement with more intention.

February check-in. If your resolutions already slipped, it doesn’t mean you lack discipline. It usually means the goal was too vague to survive real life.

Big promises sound good in January. But they unfortunately don’t survive a busy Tuesday. 

Small, repeatable habits beat dramatic resets every time. And when your plan is grounded in what your body actually needs, change becomes sustainable.
